pi_items.verified_value: Requires score ≥75 (4/5 criteria met). Criteria: (1) warmth reduction ≥30% or ≥10 units, (2) RGB variance decreased, (3) correct direction (blue↑ and red/yellow↓), (4) no overcorrection, (5) >3% pixels changed

Task design issue: setup script claims 'warm landscape image' but actual image has cool/blue cast. Verifier criterion #1 requires 'warmth reduction' but if image is already cool (negative warmth), reducing warmth further moves in opposite direction to natural color balance correction.
pi_items.source: Comparison of setup_balance_task.sh description vs. actual image RGB analysis

privileged_info_summary: The task.json file for color_balance@1 contains NO metadata field. The task uses GIMP 2.10, has a 90-second timeout, max 8 steps, and sparse reward. The input image is downloaded from Unsplash (valid URL, 1000x667 JPEG). However, there is a critical discrepancy: the setup script describes the image as having a 'warm/yellow color cast', but image analysis reveals it actually has a cool/blue color cast (RGB mean: R≈109, G≈109, B≈116, warmth≈-7 to -19). The verifier checks for warmth reduction as criterion #1, which creates a logical mismatch when the image has negative (cool) warmth instead of positive (warm) warmth. The verifier success threshold is ≥75% (4 of 5 criteria), with criteria including warmth reduction, RGB balance improvement, correct directional change, no overcorrection, and meaningful pixel-level modification.
